conditions: 6 AM—Following yesterday's blazing sunshine, an eerie yet optimistic Thursday is dawning in the Tetons thanks to the arrival of thick, moisture-laden clouds. 3 inches of wind-whipped pow resurfaced the upper elevations overnight, and periods of snow and low-elevation liquid precipitation are expected through the day. Crazy terrain features will form thanks to strong west winds that blast in the 15 to 50+ mph range. The high winds will be aided in their creative powder-plastering campaign by mild temperatures that soar close to 30 at the summit and into the mid- to upper 40s in Teton Village. Come live out life on an inclined plane, where the rational move is to go utterly insane. -Lege
